inline_comments.message: I suggest to change to deleteArchivedService
In my opinion, responses should be handled by the controller, this method should be void. You could use a ComponentException or similar to throw errors when needed.
this list of string is not used, why have it as a return?
just let the exception to be thrown. There is a org.openecomp.sdc.be.servlets.exception.ComponentExceptionMapper that will handle ComponentException thrown in an endpoint.

I suggest to change to:
Deletes an archived service

In my opinion, we should not use Either, just return the List<String>. For errors (Either.right cases), you can just throw a custom runtime or checked exception with the StorageOperationStatus (to support storing this information from sub calls with Either returns)

I recommend to just assign straight to the variable:
final Either<GraphVertex, StorageOperationStatus> componentToDelete =
before proceeding, you need to check if it is right (error or not found)
are you deleting all the services versions? You should check if any version is in use, not only the highest one.

suggest to change to:
List<String> affectedComponentIds = new ArrayList<>();

please, surround this for in a try catch finally. Include the "commitAndCheck(highestVersion.getUniqueId())" as the last line of the try.
In the catch, do a "janusGraphDao.rollback()".
Also, if any deletion fails on line 1017 "if (deleteToscaElement.isRight())", also do a rollback and return.
It would be good to lock all the services before any delete:

StorageOperationStatus storageOperationStatus = graphLockOperation.lockComponent(service.getUniqueId(), NodeTypeEnum.Service);

then release the lock in the finally.

related to the suggestion on line 1014:
affectedComponentIds.add(elementV.getUniqueId());

related to the suggestion on line 1014:
Either.left(affectedComponentIds)
avoid to use abbreviation for better clarity: vertex or graphVertex
avoid using Either, just return a List<GraphVertex>
why use a map if you are returning the map values?
please, unwrap from the Either to remove unnecessary complexity
with following suggestions, this variable is not needed
this whole code is only for the log.debug entry on line 1073. So, check first if the debug log is enabled with log.isDebugEnabled() before calling anything in this block.
I believe I am doing this on line 1008
I actually wanted it to iterate over all the Graph Vertices in the list so all the Graph Vertices that are in use will be logged instead of just the first one.
I was using it so it wouldn't store duplicates but, I will use a TreeSet instead
I can't change to parentVertex since parentVertex is already a variable.
I can't unwrap this since parentVertex is of type Either<GraphVertex, JanusGraphOperationStatus>
I suggest to change to deletedServices or deletedServiceList
please, check if the debug is enabled before iterating through the list
you are throwing an exception in the next line, and this will force a rollback already in the catch. I would say to just remove this line.

Ahh cool did not know you could do that with .either
Done
I though we had decide on option 1, but maybe I am mistaken?
If the service is in use the error thrown to the user should indicate it cannot be deleted because it is in use and give information on the service(s) in which it is being used
are the last 2 bullet points in the jira addressed?
no tests were added for this condition. For void methods, you can use Mockito.verify to see if it was called or not

you could get the exception and check its content (action status and params):

final ComponentException actualException = assertThrows(ComponentException.class ...

this is not a "Model" related exception. Maybe create a new one: ToscaOperationExceptionSupplier.
Also, I couldn't find tests for this exception.
why not return the Set itself?

might be better to delete the model after the service and only if the service has been successfully deleted
and commit here after the service versions and model has been deleted instead of when just the service versions are deleted
add @Parameter to document the param so the user understands how to use it
can we use the enum here instead of string to ensure valid value is passed
According to the java doc for this method it should be considered deprecated, use git fetch java.util.Objects.equals instead

Should be able to get all versions of the component using getComponentListByInvariantUuid. The invariantUuid is the same for all versions of the component
Can we then just pass all the components to checkIfInUseAndDelete then to reuse existing code rather than creating new code to do the same thing
this is not an operation on a model

Just get the parent from the edge? i.e. belongingEdgeByCriteria.left().value().inVertex()
or just go straight to get the parents without first getting the edges (i.e. remove the call to getBelongingEdgeByCriteria)
a lot of this code is duplicated in getComponentsUsingComponent, refactor to reduce code duplication
A similar approach can be taken here, extracting to a method void checkNotUsed(List<GraphVertex> vertices) or something like that
if we are committing here, shouldn't we rollback in case of any exception? That is a question also for Michael Morris.

shouldn't it be: 
Optional parameter to determine the delete action

avoid returning null for a collection return method, this helps a lot to avoid null pointer and the user can just use the .empty() instead to verify if the result is empty. Just return an empty collection with Collections.emptyList()
please, don't use wild card imports
please, extract the uniqueId to a variable. Same for other methods.
Would we also have to lock and unlock the components here as well then?
I think so, it would be safer that way

Would be better to leave also the explanation for the default value as you had here:
"Optional parameter to determine the delete action: DELETE, which will permanent delete the Service from the system or MARK_AS_DELETE, which will logically mark the service as deleted. Default action is to MARK_AS_DELETE."

the idea here is to have the same value for both always, right? If they are different, probably the test will fail. So better having this in a single variable.
The same applies for the other similar cases in this file.
The invariantUUID and serviceUid are actually supposed to be different and the tests all still pass if they are different. I will change the invariantUUID in all the tests to reflect this
all right, so, in that case, better to do as you said and have them different in the test.
